title: krameria paucifolia
intro: Krameria paucifolia, also known as Macambo or Queñua, is a plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America, belonging to the Krameria genus. The plant's roots have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to their medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions, including ulcers, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Krameria paucifolia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of infections and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the roots are typically dried and powdered, then used to make teas, infusions, or capsules for internal use, or applied topically to treat skin conditions.
